title: calea grazielae
intro: Calea grazielae, also known as the Mexican feathergrass, is a plant species native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its feathery plumes and yellow flowers. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, as well as its ability to act as a sedative and antianxiety agent. The health benefits of Calea grazielae include reducing anxiety and stress, promoting relaxation and sleep, and alleviating symptoms of insomnia and restlessness. Calea grazielae can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often used in traditional herbal remedies to promote a sense of calm and well-being.
